title: EVA MMO
wikitext:
[]: EVA MMO             Quote Post by thomson777 » Mon Sep 13, 2010 2:06 am Don't tell me this wouldn't kick ass. Well up to the point where all I see are unit ones and everyone complains about who died for their eva's soul. but other than that the pvp would be awesome especially if you could play as an angle. I could even let people create their own missions for real rewards based on what they want to protect or gain. However it would best be played in a alternate universe considering what happened in EVA it's self. If I created this mmo no one would be able to recreate any of the original characters unless they payed me first (I would like an mmo with continuity but I'm a sucker for money.) The leveling system would need some changes from traditional MMORPGs but it wouldn't be a good mmo without a lot of changes form traditional MMORPGs.

[]: Re: EVA MMO             Quote Post by Ramiel » Mon Sep 13, 2010 2:21 am thomson777 wrote: but other than that the pvp would be awesome especially if you could play as an angle.

[]:             Quote Post by Darkman.exe213 » Mon Sep 13, 2010 2:22 am I'd hate to say it, but it's already been done. http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=jo4RX1BKlVg

[]: Re: EVA MMO             Quote Post by thomson777 » Mon Sep 13, 2010 2:56 am Ramiel wrote: That said, it'd be pretty cool if there was a viable way for it to work. Good luck finding said way.

[]:             Quote Post by EvangelionFan » Mon Sep 13, 2010 3:07 am I would only play an NGE MMO if I can be one of the Reis from the Reiquarium. ...and then I would totally pick the dummy plug as my prestige class so I can watch Shinji scream as I smash Bardiel/Unit 03.

[]:             Quote Post by Ramiel » Mon Sep 13, 2010 3:23 am Darkman.exe213 wrote: I'd hate to say it, but it's already been done. http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=jo4RX1BKlVg

[]:             Quote Post by thomson777 » Mon Sep 13, 2010 3:38 am EvangelionFan wrote: I would only play an NGE MMO if I can be one of the Reis from the Reiquarium. ...and then I would totally pick the dummy plug as my prestige class so I can watch Shinji scream as I smash Bardiel/Unit 03.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Mon Sep 13, 2010 8:51 am An Evangelion MMO would never work. Evangelion barely works as a video game , let alone and MMO video game.

[]:             Quote Post by InstrumentalityOne » Mon Sep 13, 2010 10:45 am Well... how about this: The level cap is 100 Every week there is a global Angel event. The whole server has to kill an Angel with teamwork and shit. If the Angel kills everybody before it gets killed, Third Impact. If the level cap is reached, Third Impact. Then the world is reset, everbody is tanged and the whole madness begins again tomorrow. You can move around as tang and talk to people and shit while you´re waiting for the world to reset.

[]:             Quote Post by Holy Diver » Mon Sep 13, 2010 11:13 am The Eva Monkey wrote: An Evangelion MMO would never work. Evangelion barely works as a video game , let alone and MMO video game.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Mon Sep 13, 2010 11:43 am Holy Diver wrote: I disagree. IMO, I think Eva would make an excellent shooter/hack n' slash game. You could even add a multiplayer mode with a wide range of customization where you can switch up your Eva's weaponry and appearance and bash your friends into submission (a la Armored Core).

[]:             Quote Post by ran1 » Mon Sep 13, 2010 12:04 pm When I intially read this topic, I thought about the idea of having a few hundred playable NERV characters fighting the JSDF. I suppose I had the wrong idea.

[]:             Quote Post by thomson777 » Mon Sep 13, 2010 12:33 pm maybe it's just me but if an Eva mmo is made I would steer clear of any resemblance to modern mmorpgs and keep it along the lines of more innovative thinking. For example I would have three classes to start (maybe more if I find something else that looks fun in the Eva universe) commanders who play it like an mmorts, giving missions to evas and their own troops, evas who play it like a mmo third person shooter/hack and slash destroying troops, angles, or other evas while deciding alliances and weather they value human life, and angles who act as world raid bosses or raid bosses controllable by lucky characters who pay me money to do so, or are chosen by random.

[]:             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Mon Sep 13, 2010 4:33 pm Do it, but take out all the stuff involving real world money.

[]:             Quote Post by Gamer137 » Mon Sep 13, 2010 8:36 pm I guess an MMO could work the back story was just a bunch of Evas and other NERV projects inside simulations like episode three. Still think an Eva Sim City needs to be made though first.

[]:             Quote Post by EvangelionFan » Mon Sep 13, 2010 8:38 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: Yeah, maybe a multiplayer game like Evarena. But not an MMO. Once you cross over into MMO territory, you'd have dozens or hundreds of angels and Evas running around. That would completely defeat the point. That's not Evangelion.

[]:             Quote Post by Bobbk » Mon Sep 13, 2010 10:36 pm InstrumentalityOne wrote: Well... how about this: The level cap is 100 Every week there is a global Angel event. The whole server has to kill an Angel with teamwork and shit. If the Angel kills everybody before it gets killed, Third Impact. If the level cap is reached, Third Impact.

[]:             Quote Post by IrkenEvangelion » Mon Sep 13, 2010 10:46 pm Hmmm.. I think it could work like this. There would be 'cities' where only say 100 people could be in at a time (like APB). There would be different professions such as NERV Technician, Pilot, Officer, etc... Each would have a different job. An angel would attack every hour or so and all people would have to pull together and do their jobs (Technician, some kind of minigame, Pilot, actual combat, Officer, strategy /minigame of some kind.) If they won they get xp and or unlockable skills or items. If they lost then they lost a dramatic amount of xp or levels and need to spend time doing tasks such as rebuilding HQ, repairing Evas, etc. During Angel attacks they could do tasks, browse around town, work on skills, customize things. There's a quick idea. Obvious flaws but I'm brainstorming here.
